Skip to contents
papercheck 0.0.0.9013
  • Get started
  • Reference

papercheck

Source: vignettes/papercheck.Rmd
papercheck.Rmd

Installation

You can install the development version of papercheck from GitHub with:

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("scienceverse/papercheck")
library(papercheck)

Load from PDF

The function pdf2grobid() can read PDF files and save them in the TEI format created by grobid. This requires an internet connection and takes a few seconds per paper, so should only be done once and the results saved for later use.

If the server is unavailable, you can use a grobid web interface.

grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package="papercheck")
pdf_file <- file.path(grobid_dir, "incest.pdf")
xml_file <- pdf2grobid(pdf_file)

Load from XML

The function read_grobid() can read XML files parsed by grobid.

s <- read_grobid(xml_file)

Full Text

You can access a parsed table of the full text of the paper via s$full_text, but you may find it more convenient to use the function search_text(). The defaults return a data table of each sentence, with the section type, header, div, paragraph and sentence numbers, and file name. (The section type is a best guess from the headers, so may not always be accurate.)

text <- search_text(s)
text section header div p s file
Moral opposition to incest is thought to play an important role in preventing inbreeding. abstract Abstract 0 1 1 incest.xml
Inbreeding is damaging to reproductive fitness. intro Introduction 1 1 1 incest.xml
Participants 1998 participants took part in this online study (see online supplemental materials, https://osf.io/mwzuq/). method Methods 2 1 1 incest.xml
Participants completed the same moral wrongness task that had been used by Lieberman and colleagues. method Procedure 3 1 1 incest.xml
Following Lieberman et al. method Analysis 4 1 1 incest.xml
Our main, omnibus analysis showed a significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). results Results 5 1 1 incest.xml
We found that having other-sex siblings is associated with greater moral opposition to sibling incest. discussion Discussion 6 1 1 incest.xml

Pattern

You can search for a specific word or phrase by setting the pattern argument. The pattern is a regex string by default; set fixed = TRUE if you want to find exact text matches.

text <- search_text(s, pattern = "signific")
text section header div p s file
Our main, omnibus analysis showed a significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). results Results 5 1 1 incest.xml
Our analysis of moral wrongness scores for sibling incest scores showed a significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.559, t(578) = -2.84, p = 0.005). results Results 5 2 1 incest.xml
In contrast, our analysis of moral wrongness scores for parental incest scores showed no significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.268, t(578) = -1.373, p = 0.17). results Results 5 2 3 incest.xml
In contrast, having other-sex siblings was not significantly associated with greater moral opposition to parent-child incest. discussion Discussion 6 2 1 incest.xml

Section

Set section to a vector of the sections to search in.

text <- search_text(s, "signific", 
                    section = "results")
text section header div p s file
Our main, omnibus analysis showed a significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). results Results 5 1 1 incest.xml
Our analysis of moral wrongness scores for sibling incest scores showed a significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.559, t(578) = -2.84, p = 0.005). results Results 5 2 1 incest.xml
In contrast, our analysis of moral wrongness scores for parental incest scores showed no significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.268, t(578) = -1.373, p = 0.17). results Results 5 2 3 incest.xml

Return

Set return to one of “sentence”, “paragraph”, “section”, or “match” to control what gets returned.

text <- search_text(s, "signific", 
                    section = "results", 
                    return = "paragraph")
text section header div p s file
Our main, omnibus analysis showed a significant three-way interaction among incest type, participant sex, and sibling type (Estimate = 0.285, t(578.003) = 2.359, p = 0.019). This interaction is illustrated in Figure 1. Next we analyzed scores for sibling and parent-child incest separately. results Results 5 1 NA incest.xml
Our analysis of moral wrongness scores for sibling incest scores showed a significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.559, t(578) = -2.84, p = 0.005). Women with brothers only tended to view sibling incest more negatively than did women with sisters only (Estimate = 0.179, t(452) = 2.022, p = 0.044) while men with brothers only tended to view sibling incest less negatively than did men with sisters only (Estimate = -0.38, t(126) = -1.933, p = 0.055). In contrast, our analysis of moral wrongness scores for parental incest scores showed no significant interaction between participant sex and sibling type (Estimate = -0.268, t(578) = -1.373, p = 0.17). results Results 5 2 NA incest.xml

Regex matches

You can also return just the matched text from a regex search by setting return = "match". The extra ... arguments in search_text() are passed to grep(), so perl = TRUE allows you to use more complex regex, like below.

pattern <- "[^\\s\\(\\)]+\\s*(=|<)\\s*[0-9\\.-]+"
text <- search_text(s, pattern, return = "match", perl = TRUE)
text section header div p s file
N=313 abstract Abstract 0 1 4 incest.xml
N=269 abstract Abstract 0 1 4 incest.xml
Estimate = 0.285 results Results 5 1 1 incest.xml
p = 0.019 results Results 5 1 1 incest.xml
Estimate = -0.559 results Results 5 2 1 incest.xml
p = 0.005 results Results 5 2 1 incest.xml
Estimate = 0.179 results Results 5 2 2 incest.xml
p = 0.044 results Results 5 2 2 incest.xml
Estimate = -0.38 results Results 5 2 2 incest.xml
p = 0.055 results Results 5 2 2 incest.xml
Estimate = -0.268 results Results 5 2 3 incest.xml
p = 0.17 results Results 5 2 3 incest.xml

Batch Processing

The functions pdf2grobid() and read_grobid() also work on a folder of files, returning a list of XML file paths or scienceverse study objects, respectively. search_text() also works on a list of study objects.

grobid_dir <- system.file("grobid", package="papercheck")

studies <- read_grobid(grobid_dir)

hypotheses <- search_text(studies, "hypothesi", 
                          section = "intro", 
                          return = "paragraph")

Modules 

module_list()
#>              name                 title type
#> 1    ai-summarise    Summarise Sections   ai
#> 2    all-p-values     List All P-Values text
#> 3        marginal Marginal Significance text
#> 4       osf-links       Check OSF Links code
#> 5 retractionwatch       RetractionWatch code
#>                                                                      path
#> 1    /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/ai-summarise.json
#> 2    /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/all-p-values.json
#> 3        /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/marginal.json
#> 4       /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/osf-links.json
#> 5 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/papercheck/modules/retractionwatch.json
p <- module_run(studies, "all-p-values")

head(p)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 7
#>   text      section header     div     p     s file        
#>   <chr>     <chr>   <chr>    <dbl> <dbl> <int> <chr>       
#> 1 p = 0.012 intro   [div-01]     1     4     6 eyecolor.xml
#> 2 p = 0.849 intro   [div-01]     1     4     7 eyecolor.xml
#> 3 p = 0.019 intro   [div-01]     1     4     8 eyecolor.xml
#> 4 p = 0.879 intro   [div-01]     1     4     9 eyecolor.xml
#> 5 p = 0.266 intro   [div-01]     1     4    11 eyecolor.xml
#> 6 p = 0.403 intro   [div-01]     1     4    13 eyecolor.xml

ChatGPT

You can query the extracted text of papers with chatGPT.

Setup

Run

gpt_setup()

You will need to provide your own API key. You can enter it manually for each call to gpt(), or add the following lines in the .Renviron file for your user or project.

CHATGPT_KEY="sk-proj-abcdefghijklmnopqrs0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRS"
RETICULATE_PYTHON="~/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/bin/python"
# useful if you aren't sure where this file is
usethis::edit_r_environ()

GPT Queries 

query <- "What is the hypothesis of this study?"
gpt_hypo <- gpt(hypotheses, query)
file answer cost
eyecolor.xml The hypothesis of this study is to test the matching hypothesis, sex-linked heritable preference hypothesis, and positive sexual imprinting hypothesis in relation to eye color and partner selection. 0.000612
incest.xml The hypothesis of this study is that humans possess adaptations to reduce inbreeding, and that the strong moral opposition to incest plays an important role in preventing inbreeding. 0.000216
prereg.xml The hypothesis of this study is that more efficient study designs might increase the willingness to pre-register in psychological science. 0.000608

On this page
